results.title: userTotalStaked invariant will be broken due to vulnerable implementations in release()
results.reasoning: Yes. Audit finding #4 describes the exact same issue: in release(), stake amounts are increased but userTotalStaked is not, after slash() previously decremented it. This breaks the userTotalStaked invariant and causes withdraw functions to underflow and revert, permanently locking the released funds. The code path (release() affecting later withdraws), mechanism (accounting desync of userTotalStaked), impact, and fix (increment userTotalStaked in release()) match the vulnerability description. Fixing release() to update userTotalStaked would resolve both.
